original: Eutychius is deposed as Patriarch of Constantinople by John Scholasticus
Military triumph of Wak Jalam Chan (Lady Six Sky), female ruler of the Maya city of Sa’aal (Naranjo) as depicted on Stela 24 Naranjo
Battle of Basing: Danish invasion army beats Saxon Ethelred of Wessex
Great comet of 1471 (C/1471 Y1) becomes the closest comet in modern times, coming within 10 million kilometers of Earth
The first contingent of 150 Swiss Guards arrives at the Vatican
Jews are expelled from Colmar, Alsace
Ottoman forces take Cairo, capital of the Mamluk Sultanate
Pope Sixtus V decrees "Immense aeterni" (Reformed curia)
Postal service between New York & Boston inaugurated
Lord Halifax becomes Speaker of English House of Lords
Iroquois tribes renew allegiance to British against French
Russian troops occupy Königsberg, East Prussia [OS=Jan 11]
Battle at Wandewash India: British troops beat French
Spain cedes the Falkland Islands to Britain
Marshal Oscar von Lubomirski confiscates goods being sold by Jews in settlements on the outskirts of Warsaw, Poland
Coup d'état in Batavian Republic
Americans capture Frenchtown, Michigan Territory
First Knights Templar Grand Encampment in the US is held in New York City
British freighter Diana sinks off Malaya
Ashantis defeat British forces in the Gold Coast
Earthquake in southern Syria kills thousands
New Zealand Company settlers arrive aboard the Aurora at Te Whanganui a Tara, which becomes Port Nicholson, Wellington
Alta California becomes a daily paper, 1st such in Calif
National Association of Base Ball Players is founded in New York
Confederate government raises premium for volunteers from $10 to $20
January Uprising begins in Poland, prompted a rebellion against conscription into the Russian army
The January Uprising, an insurrection in Russia's Kingdom of Poland aimed at restoration of the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth begins; carries on for 1-1/2 years, but fails
Union General Burnside's "Mud March"
British SS Northfleet sinks at Dungeness, England, 300 die
Arthur Tooth, an Anglican clergyman is taken into custody after being prosecuted for using ritualist practices
James Shields (D) elected US senator from Missouri after previously serving as US senator from Illinois & Minnesota
Zulu warriors attack British Army camp in Isandhlwana, South Africa
"Cleopatra's Needle", a 3,500-year-old Ancient Egyptian obelisk is erected in Central Park, New York
England completes 1st innings victory in Tests vs. Australia at Melbourne Cricket Grounds
Columbia Phonograph is formed in Washington, D.C.
Jose Marti forms La Liga (Union of Cuban exiles) in NYC
National Association of Manufacturers organized in Cincinnati
Leaders of six Australian colonies meet in Melbourne to discuss confederation
The Hay-Herran Treaty concerning the USA's right to the Panama Canal is signed by the Colombian Charge d'affaires in Washington, D.C. (never ratified)
SS Valencia runs aground on rocks on Vancouver Island, British Columbia, killing more than 130

Katie Mulcahey is arrested for lighting a cigarette, violating the one-day-old "Sullivan Ordinance" banning women from smoking in public, and is fined $5. Appearing before the judge, she states, “I’ve got as much right to smoke as you have. I never heard of this new law, and I don’t want to hear about it. No man shall dictate to me.”
Vassily Kandinsky forms Kunstlerverein in Munich
Opera "Germania" premieres in NYC
Paul Claudel's play "L'échange" premieres in Paris
Ukraine proclaimed a free republic (German puppet)
Dutch Blast Furnace & Steel Factory opens
KGO-AM in San Francisco CA begins radio transmissions
Albania Republic proclaimed under President Achmed Zogu
Belgian chief of staff General Maglinse quits
-35°F (-37°C), Mount Carroll, Illinois (state record)
French government of Steeg falls
Isaac Isaacs is sworn in as the first Australian-born Governor-General of Australia
Jazz trumpeter Clyde McCoy records "Sugar Blues" for Columbia Records; sells over 14 million copies internationally
VARA begins experimental TV broadcast in Diamantbeurs Amsterdam
Aquatic Park, near Fisherman's Wharf, San Francisco dedicated
First radio broadcast of "Road to Happiness" on CBS
British and Australian troops capture Tobruk from Italians
Sietze de Groot wins 8th Dutch 11 city skate (8:44:06)
66.34 cm (26.12") precipitation over 24 hour period in Hoegees Camp, California (state record)
Allied Joint Chiefs of Staff determine invasion of Sicily for July 10th
Temperature rises 49°F (9°C) in 2 minutes in Spearfish, South Dakota
Allied forces begin landing at Anzio on the Italian mainland
Burma highway reopens
Heavy US air raid on Okinawa
First commercial TV station west of Mississippi, KTLA (channel 5) in Los Angeles, California, begins broadcasting
Chinatown telephone exchange in San Francisco is closed
Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded to Johncrowe Ransom
1953 NFL Draft: Harry Babcock from University of Georgia first pick by San Francisco 49ers
30 people die in a train crash in Los Angeles
Canadian Football Council forms
Mad Bomber (George P. Metesky) is accused of 30 explosions and arrested
KRSD (now KEVN) TV channel 7 in Rapid City, SD (ABC) 1st broadcast
USAF concludes that less than 1% of UFOs are unknown objects
Paul Pender defeats Sugar Ray Robinson for the middleweight boxing title
Organization of American States suspends Cuba's membership
Kenneth Kaunda becomes the first President of Northern Rhodesia (Zambia)
World's largest cheese (15,723 kg) is manufactured in Wisconsin for the New York World's Fair
US launches TIROS 9 weather satellite
"Rowan & Martin's Laugh-In" sketch comedy television program premieres on NBC
NASA's Apollo 5: First unmanned test flight of Apollo Lunar Module is launched in earth orbit by Saturn 1B.
NBA announces expansion franchise licenses awarded to Milwaukee Bucks and Phoenix Suns
Orbiting Solar Observatory 5 launched into earth orbit
First commercial Boeing 747 flight, Pan American World Airways flies from New York City to London in 6½ hours

An anti-internment march is held at Magilligan strand, County Derry; as the march nears the internment camp it is stopped by members of the Green Jackets and the Parachute Regiment of the British Army, who used barbed wire to close off the beach
US, North Vietnam, South Vietnam, and Vietcong sign boundary accord
Earth Resources Technology Satellite Landsat 2 is launched
Bank robbery in Beirut nets a record $20 to $50 million
10,000s UK public sector workers go on strike
O A "Bum" Phillips becomes head coach of NFL New Orleans Saints
75% of North America is covered by snow
Houston is 1st NBA team to be held scoreless in an overtime Portland Trail Blazers out score them 17-0 & win 113-96
Second flight readiness firing of Challenger's main engines lasts 22 seconds
Annette Kennedy of SUNY sets women's college basketball record with 70 points in 116-21 victory over Pratt Institute at Purchase, New York
Hilbert van de Thumb becomes European Allround speedskating champion
Super Bowl XVIII, Tampa Stadium, Tampa, FL: LA Raiders beat Washington Redskins, 38-9; MVP: Marcus Allen, Los Angeles, RB
-30°F (-34°C), Mountain Lake Bio Station, Virginia (state record)
Cold wave damages 90% of Florida's citrus crop
Ghorbanifar 1st suggests diversion of cash to contras, says North
Blizzard in NJ, as 334 attend Devils-Flame NHL game, NJ wins 7-5
Pennsylvania politician R. Budd Dwyer shoots and kills himself at a press conference on live national television, leading to debates about boundaries in journalism
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Robert Tappan Morris is convicted of releasing the 1988 Internet worm, or Morris worm, one of the oldest computer worms distributed via the Internet
Will Clark, NL's MVP signs a $15M 4-year contract with SF Giants
Cholera epidemic begins in Peru with first known sufferer, 300,000 people infected over the next 12 months
Rebel forces occupy Zaire's national radio station in Kinshasa and broadcast a demand for the government's resignation
Sarah, Duchess of York (aka Fergie) wears paper bag over her head on airline ride
Space Shuttle STS-42 (Discovery 15) launches into space
Johan Koss skates a world record 5 km in 6:38.77
5.5 magnitude earthquake strikes Sumatra
Palestinian bomb attack in Beit Lid Israel, 21-22 killed
Space shuttle Atlantis successfully returns to Earth
NHL's Minnesota franchise selects the nickname Wild
STS 89 (Endeavour 12) launches into orbit
World League of American Football renamed as NFL Europe
Australian missionary Graham Staines and his two sons are burned alive by radical Hindus while sleeping in their car in Eastern India
The British government launches pro-vaccine campaign
Kmart Corp becomes the largest retailer in United States history to file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection
The jury portion of the trial against Robert Pickton, accused of being Canada's worst serial killer, opens in New Westminster, British Columbia
A series of Baghdad car bombings kill 17 people
The Bank of Japan doubles its inflation target to 2% and announces open-ended asset purchases for 2014 in the hope of ending deflation
Water vapour is detected on the dwarf planet Ceres
Winter storm conditions strand 500 motorists for 24 hours in Somerset and Bedford counties, about 80 miles southeast of Pittsburgh, on the Pennsylvania Turnpike
"Call Me By Your Name", directed by Luca Guadagnino and starring Timothée Chalamet, premieres at the Sundance Film Festival
Chile declares a State of Emergency and requests international assistance as wildfires rage out of control
Netflix becomes the largest digital media and entertainment company in the world worth $100 billion
US government ends three-day shutdown after an agreement in Congress to extend funding
Lloyd Austin confirmed as defense secretary by the US Senate, and the first black head of the Pentagon
Australian surfer Laura Enever sets world record riding the largest-ever wave by a woman at 43.6ft (13.3m) on Oahu's North Shore, Hawaii
China's northernmost city, Mohe, records record low temperature of -53C (-63F)
Chinese Year of the Rabbit begins with China marking the largest celebration of the New Year since 2020, after Covid-zero restrictions lifted
World's first mass vaccine program against Malaria begins in Yaoundé, Cameroon, after successful trials of RTS,S vaccine showed it saved one in three lives
England and France declare war on Holy Roman EmperorCharles V
EnglishQueen Elizabeth Igrants composers Thomas Tallis & William Byrd music press monopoly
Prince William of Orange(future KingWilliam IIIof Britain) summons the Convention Parliament to discuss ruling jointly with his wife Mary, the daughter of exiled KingJames II
Lord Byroncompletes poems "Parisina" and "Siege of Corinth"
Charles Darwintakes his Bachelors of Art exam at Christ's College, Cambridge, coming tenth out of 171 candidates
Charles Dickensarrives in Boston, Massachusetts with his wife, Catherine
Johannes Brahms' 1st piano concerto (in D minor) premieres in Hanover
After 63 years Britain stops sale ofQueen Victoriapostage stamps series & begins KingEdward VIIseries
Dmitri Shostakovich's opera "Lady Macbeth of the Mtsensk District" debuts in Leningrad
"Our Town",Thornton Wilder's Pulitzer-winning stage drama of small-town life in Grover's Corners, New Hampshire, premieres at McCarter Theatre in Princeton, New Jersey
Fidel Castrois ejected from a Winter League baseball game after hitting a batter
Arthur Miller's play "The Crucible" premieres at the Martin Beck Theater on Broadway in NYC (1953 Tony for Best Play)

Buddy Hollymakes his last recordings in his NYC apartment, alone with an acoustic guitar; he tapes five songs including "Peggy Sue" and "Crying, Waiting, Hoping", which were embellished, overdubbed, and released posthumously by Coral Records
Elysée treaty of cooperation between France and Germany signed byCharles de Gaulleand Konrad Adenauer
"Lady Soul" 14th studio album byAretha Franklinis released (Billboard Album of the Year, 1968)
Roy CampanellaandStan Musialelected to baseball Hall of Fame
John Lennonand Plastic Ono Band record "Power to the People" at his Ascot Sound home studio at Tittenhurst Park, England
George ForemanTKOsJoe Frazierin 2 rounds to win WBC & WBA heavyweight boxing titles in Kingston, Jamaica; Frazier knocked down 3 times in both 1st & 2nd rounds

In a landmark decision, the US Supreme Court legalizes most abortions (Roe v. Wade). Authoring the majority opinion,Harry Blackmunstates that the criminalization of abortion does not have "roots in the English common-law tradition."
Russian dissidentsAndrei SakharovandYelena Bonnerarrested in Moscow and banished to Gorky
Apple's seminal ad for the newMacintoshcomputer, directed byRidley Scott, loosely based onGeorge Orwell's "1984", plays during the Super Bowl
Defending championMike Tysonbeats former titleholderLarry Holmesby TKO in round 4 at Convention Centre, Atlantic City to retain his undisputed heavyweight boxing title
After winning his third Super Bowl as head coach of the San Francisco 49ers,Bill Walshretires
Super Bowl XXIII, Joe Robbie Stadium, Miami, FL: San Francisco 49ers beat Cincinnati Bengals, 20-16; MVP:Jerry Rice, SF, WR
PresidentMikhail Gorbachevsends Red Troops into the Soviet republic of Azerbaijan
MLB outfielderRickey Henderson, rejoins Oakland A's for his 4th and final stint with the team
Evo Moralesis inaugurated as President of Bolivia, becoming the country's first indigenous president
Kobe Bryantscores 81 points in a 122-104 Lakers victory over the Toronto Raptors; second-highest game total in NBA history, behind only Wilt Chamberlain's 100-point game (1962)
Conan O'Brien's last The Tonight Show episode after a big controversy over the Tonight Show timeslot
Jared Kushneris sworn in as Senior Advisor to US President,Donald Trump

New Orleans Pelicans'DeMarcus Cousinshas 44 points, 24 rebounds & 10 assists in 132-128 double-OT win over Chicago Bulls; 1st player sinceKareem Abdul-Jabbar(1972) with 40+ points, 20+ rebounds & 10+ assists
Joel Embiidsets NBA 76ers franchise record with 70 points in a single game during 133-123 win over visiting San Antonio Spurs, in Philadelphia; previous record of 68 set byWilt Chamberlainin 1967
New Hindu temple to the god Ram inaugurated by Indian PMNarendra Modiin Ayodhya city in a grand ceremony, on site of a mosque razed to the ground by Hindi militants in 1992
Parts of Switzerland adopt Gregorian calendar (& parts in 1812)
Battle of Rorke's Drift: British garrison of 150 holds off 3,000-4,000 Zulu warriors. Eleven Victoria Crosses and several other decorations are awarded to the defenders

In St Petersburg, Russia, a large demonstration of workers led by Father Gapon, march to the Winter Palace with a petition to the Tsar; troops fire on protesters in what becomes known as 'Bloody Sunday'
Kuwaiti oil facilities are destroyed by Iraqi forces
